text.title: RAG Evaluation Workflow and Agent Integration
text.summary: Four-step evaluation agent workflow, RAGAS usage notes, production monitoring setup, and common pitfalls.

rule.statement: Separate retrieval evaluation from generation evaluation in every run; a combined score masks root cause and misdirects fixes.
rule.rationale: Retrieval and generation have independent failure modes. A high faithfulness score with low hit rate means the model hallucinates consistently from bad retrieval — not a generation problem.

list.item: Step 1: Load evaluation dataset (query, expected_answer, source_doc_ids)
Step 2: Run RAG pipeline — get retrieved_chunk_ids and generated_answer
Step 3: Compute retrieval metrics (Precision@K, Hit Rate, MRR) + generation metrics via LLM-as-judge
Step 4: Write structured report with per-metric scores, failure examples, and recommendations

rule.statement: Target these baselines before production: Faithfulness > 0.85, Answer Relevance > 0.90, Hit Rate > 0.90.
rule.rationale: These thresholds represent consistent observed quality in production RAG systems across diverse domains.

list.item: Development: evaluate on every change, full metric suite
Production: continuous lightweight sampling (faithfulness + hit rate); full suite monthly
Run evaluation in CI; block merge if faithfulness drops >5% vs main

rule.statement: Generate synthetic ground truth (question-answer pairs) with a different model than the one being evaluated.
rule.rationale: Using the same model to generate and evaluate creates self-fulfilling evaluations — the evaluated model scores artificially high on its own synthetic questions.

list.item: Manually verify 100 annotated query-document pairs minimum
Include diverse question types: factual, comparative, multi-hop reasoning
Cover edge cases and out-of-domain queries

antipattern.description: RAGAS context_recall used without ground truth answers.
antipattern.reason: context_recall requires ground_truth to compute; without it the metric is unavailable. Check dataset completeness before running.

antipattern.description: Running RAGAS evaluation of 100 samples without rate limiting.
antipattern.reason: RAGAS faithfulness makes multiple LLM calls per sample (claim extraction + support checking); 100 samples costs $5–20 and hits rate limits if parallelized without a semaphore.

antipattern.description: Reporting only a single-run RAGAS score without confidence intervals.
antipattern.reason: LLM-as-judge scores vary ±3–5% between runs. Single-point estimates mislead stakeholders about actual quality.
